data.title: Candel Therapeutics GAAP EPS of -$0.54
data.description:
• Candel Therapeutics press release (CADL): Q4 GAAP EPS of -$0.54.

  • Cash Position: Cash and cash equivalents, as of December 31, 2025, were $119.7 million compared to $102.7 million as of December 31, 2024. Based on current operating plans, the Company expects that its existing cash and cash equivalents, as of December 31, 2025, together with proceeds from the follow-on equity offering in February 2026, will be sufficient to fund operations into Q1 2028.

data.title: Investor pessimism deepens as bearish sentiment climbs, shows latest AAII survey
data.description:
Market sentiment among individual investors turned more pessimistic in mid-March, according to the latest survey from the American Association of Individual Investors.

Bullish sentiment, or expectations that stock prices will rise over the next six months, slipped to 31.9% for the week ended March 11, down from 33.1% in the prior week, while bearish sentiment climbed sharply to 46.4% from 35.5% a week earlier.

Equity markets experienced heightened volatility during the week as escalating conflict involving Iran pushed oil prices higher and rattled investor confidence. U.S. stocks slid at several points, with the S&P 500 pressured by concerns over potential energy supply disruptions linked to the Strait of Hormuz.

Oil prices surged during the period, at one point approaching $100 per barrel amid tanker attacks and shipping risks in the region, while Treasury yields moved higher as investors monitored inflation data and broader economic signals. Rising energy costs and geopolitical uncertainty also fueled recession fears in some corners of the market.

According to AAII, neutral sentiment, or expectations that stock prices will stay essentially unchanged over the next six months, also dropped during the week, with the figure standing at 21.7%, compared to 31.4% last week.

The survey has been conducted by the American Association of Individual Investors since 1987, in which it asks respondents for their thoughts on where the market is heading in the next six months.

S&P 500 Tracking Funds: (MUTF:FXAIX), (MUTF:VFIAX), (MUTF:VFFSX), (MUTF:SWPPX), (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS) and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).

data.title: Medtronic plc GAAP EPS of -$1.11, revenue of $33.48M
data.description:
• Medtronic plc press release (MDT): FY GAAP EPS of -$1.11.
  • Revenue of $33.48M.
  • Research and development expenses for 2025 were $58.2 million, compared with $42.8 million for 2024, which represents an increase of 36%. The increase was primarily due to additional costs associated with the ongoing BACKBEAT global pivotal study and to advance the Virtue SAB program, including the Virtue Trial.
  • Selling, general and administrative expenses for 2025 were $26.9 million, compared with $23.9 million for 2024, which represents an increase of 12%. The increase was primarily due to an increase in professional fees.

data.title: Verano outlines $195M refinancing and targets growth in Texas and Virginia amid regulatory catalysts
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Verano Holdings Corp. (VRNO:CA) Q4 2025

Management View

  • George Archos, Founder, Chairman & CEO, highlighted the completion of a $195 million senior secured term loan, stating the funds, combined with cash on hand and a $50 million draw from an existing revolving credit facility, were used to repay previous 2022 credit agreement obligations. Archos stated, "We believe our new credit facility provides Verano some of the best beneficial terms in the industry relative to peers that have announced similar agreements, including a favorable current interest rate of 9.5%, along with maturity date and prepayment flexibility."
  • Archos outlined operational improvements, including automation and technology upgrades, partnerships, and product launches that solidified Verano’s #3 national market share. He added, "We strategically expanded our footprint by opening 7 new dispensaries in 4 states and enhanced our retail presence through targeted store relocations and refreshes."
  • The company completed redomiciling to Nevada, settled litigation with Vireo, and won a conditional vertical license in Texas. Archos emphasized, "We are uniquely positioned as the only operator with current and planned operations across 4 key markets primed for expansion, Texas, Virginia, Pennsylvania and Florida."
  • Richard Tarapchak, Chief Financial Officer, stated, "Revenue was $207 million, an increase of 2% sequentially...driven by retail gains and the decrease versus the prior year period was primarily due to ongoing price compression and a slight decrease in third-party wholesale sales."

Outlook

  • Archos projected opening approximately 5 to 10 new dispensaries in 2026, primarily in Florida, and noted, "We anticipate opening approximately 5 to 10 new dispensaries in 2026, primarily in Florida and introducing additional dispensary enhancements across our footprint."
  • Tarapchak provided guidance on margins, stating, "We anticipate slightly softer margins in the first quarter, ranging from between 24% to 26%, which we believe will stabilize and improve throughout 2026."
  • The company is preparing for catalysts including potential Schedule III designation and adult-use launches in Virginia and Texas, with operations in Texas anticipated to commence in 2027.

Financial Results

  • Verano reported $207 million in Q4 2025 revenue, up $4 million sequentially, and $822 million for the full year.
  • Retail revenue reached $170 million in Q4, up $6 million from Q3, with a more than 15% sales gain in Florida over the prior quarter and year.
  • Wholesale revenue was $84 million in Q4, with vertical wholesale sales increasing but offset by lower third-party sales. Full-year wholesale revenue was $318 million.
  • Gross profit was $106 million (51% of revenue), and adjusted EBITDA was $56 million (27% of revenue). Net loss in Q4 was $183 million, reflecting over $175 million in impairment charges.
  • CapEx for Q4 was $9 million, with full year at $41 million. The company ended the quarter with $83 million in cash and $14 million in Q4 operating cash flow.
  • SG&A costs were $86 million for the quarter, up 7% sequentially due to strategic marketing but down $16 million for the full year versus 2024.

Q&A

  • Aaron Grey, Alliance Global Partners, asked about Texas expansion plans. Archos responded, "We plan on expanding throughout the state. We'd like to -- we're looking for facilities currently. We do need to get past the diligence phase...Once we do that, we plan on doing something similar we did to Florida."
  • Grey inquired about Virginia readiness. Archos replied, "We've already been expanding in Virginia...adding additional canopy. We're also making some improvements at the retail stores...We are excited for the opportunity to come."
  • William Kirk, ROTH Capital Partners, questioned the hemp ban and cost savings. Archos said, "We don't believe that there will be legislation that will delay or will rescind what's currently been put in place...the government is looking for regulated industries that provide safe product consistently."
  • Tarapchak added regarding cost savings, "We haven't stopped looking at opportunities to reduce costs from an SG&A perspective...you'll start seeing those type of savings through 2026."
  • Brenna Cunnington, ATB Cormark Capital Markets, asked about Florida market share and illicit market trends. Archos responded, "It is still a very strong medical market where we have a strong position, and we intend to keep that position and keep moving forward."
  • Neal Gilmer, Haywood Securities, inquired about gross margin sustainability and automation. Tarapchak said, "There's still opportunity here...from an automation perspective that will help us improve margins."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts’ tone focused on expansion opportunities, regulatory catalysts, and cost savings, with a generally positive but probing approach, seeking specifics on execution and market dynamics.
  • Management maintained a confident and optimistic tone, emphasizing operational achievements, cost control, and readiness for regulatory catalysts. Archos used phrases such as "We are uniquely positioned" and "We stand poised and ready to capitalize." Tarapchak’s remarks also reflected confidence about further efficiency gains.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, the tone was more forward-looking and growth-oriented, with less focus on risk mitigation and more on strategic opportunities.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter saw the announcement of a $195 million refinancing, compared to the previous quarter’s $75 million revolving credit facility.
  • Guidance language became more specific regarding new store openings and readiness for state and federal regulatory shifts.
  • Gross profit and adjusted EBITDA margins improved quarter-over-quarter, while the net loss widened due to substantial impairment charges.
  • SG&A showed a sequential increase in Q4, attributed to marketing, but was down year-over-year, reflecting ongoing cost control efforts.
  • Analysts’ focus shifted from immediate cost and margin pressures to questions about execution in new and expanding markets, particularly Texas and Virginia.
  • Management’s confidence in operational and regulatory positioning increased, with repeated references to catalysts and market readiness.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited ongoing price compression and competition as key challenges, particularly in wholesale.
  • Industry-wide payment and credit issues persist but have improved, with reinstated accounts and lower receivables.
  • Potential delays in regulatory changes at state or federal levels were noted, but management expressed confidence in readiness for legislative shifts.
  • Analysts highlighted concerns about hemp market regulation and illicit market impacts, particularly in Florida and other states.

Final Takeaway

Verano Holdings Corp. emphasized strengthened financial flexibility with a new $195 million term loan, ongoing operational efficiencies, and a strategic focus on expansion into high-potential markets such as Texas and Virginia. Management underscored readiness for regulatory catalysts and continued SG&A discipline, while highlighting robust retail and wholesale performance in key markets and a strong national brand position. The company views the coming year as pivotal amid expected federal and state policy changes.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Repsol double-upgraded at RBC, expecting refining margins to stay elevated through 2026
data.description:
Repsol (REPYY) (REPYF) +2.1% in Thursday's trading as RBC Capital double-upgraded the Spanish oil and gas producer to Outperform from Underperform with a €25 price target, raised from €20, at RBC Capital, which expects refining margins to stay elevated throughout 2026 even with disruption from the Iran war.

RBC analyst Biraj Borkhataria said his prior Sell-equivalent rating was based on a normalizing
refining complex applying downward pressure on margins, but recent events suggest 2026 is set to be anything but "normal," with significant disruption to the Middle Eastern refining complex setting the stage for material inventory draws.

These pressures are most acute on the distillate front, Borkhataria wrote, which suggests that even a short-lived Middle East war could sustain upward pressure on refining margins; the analyst said combining this with Repsol's (REPYY) (REPYF) recent strategy update, which incorporates improving free cash flow and lower capital intensity, leaves him more constructive.

data.title: Levi Strauss sees major upside for its premium Blue Tab label
data.description:
Levi Strauss & Co. (LEVI) discussed its strategic goals for the emerging Blue Tab collection during its presentation at the UBS Global Consumer and Retail Conference. 

Blue Tab, which is Levi’s newest premium denim line, is the brand’s most elevated offering and is aimed at consumers willing to pay materially more for craftsmanship, fabric quality, and fashion-forward fits. It revives an old concept, as the original Blue Tab appeared in the 1950s on the "Denim Family" line, a coordinated family collection that notably used blue tags instead of the now-iconic red flag. While the earlier incarnation faded as Levi's (ELVI) moved into mass production, the modern relaunch repurposes the Blue Tab name as a luxury platform, blending American workwear heritage with Japanese-influenced selvedge and detailing. Blue Tab is seen by analysts as underscoring Levi's (LEVI) ambition to move deeper into the high-end denim space.

The premium denim line is expected to be a key driver of long‑term value, as management sharpens the company’s focus on premiumization, higher average unit revenue, and brand elevation. Levi's (LEVI) management described the Blue Tab business as a distinct, incremental growth engine targeting a largely underpenetrated premium-denim segment, with early sell‑through in launch markets reportedly running ahead of internal expectations. 

For investors, the main takeaway is the Blue Tab could represent a shift into higher-margin products, supported by direct-to-consumer channels and selective wholesale partners.

data.title: Red Cat CEO says drone attack on Calif. could be easily intercepted
data.description:
The CEO of drone maker Red Cat (RCAT) said the U.S. military would likely be able to easily intercept a drone attack by Iran on California, even if the drones were launched from small boats, according to Fox Business.

"We don't have any details currently, but if they're trying to launch vertical-launch mechanisms off of small boats off the coast of California, it's going to be very easy to find and very easy to kill," Jeffrey Thompson told Fox Business on Thursday. 

Thompson added, however, that smaller drones, which can operate at lower altitudes, would be harder to identify and intercept.

"The really small ones like FPV drones… are going to be very hard to battle because they're just so quick," Thompson said.

Thompson's remarks come in the wake of reports that Iranian or pro-Iranian entities could be readying a drone attack on California.

data.title: U.S. stocks sink as war against Iran intensifies, oil prices keep climbing
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages were lower on Thursday as oil prices continued to climb due to the escalating U.S.-Israel-Iran conflict.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last -1.2%, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -1.5%, and the Dow Jones (DJI) was -1.3%.

Over in the bond market, the benchmark 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was 2 basis points higher at 4.25%, while the 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) rose 9 basis points to 3.75%.

Crude oil (CL1:COM) rose 8.3% to $94 per barrel on Thursday, while Brent (CO1:COM) was also higher at $99.

“I find it hard not to have a central scenario where oil prices will remain very high for a long time, higher than the market current prices,” said Olivier Blanchard, Robert Solow Professor of economics emeritus at MIT.

“There is no reason, whether or not Trump declares that war is over, to think that Iran will not continue for some time to threaten to destroy the ships that try. Why should they stop? The risk will thus remain sufficiently high that most non-Iranian ships will not take the risk. Thus, the shortfall of 20 million barrels a day is likely to last for long,” he added.

The conflict in the Middle East has triggered one of the largest supply shocks of global oil markets in history, leading to disruption of almost 7.5% of the worldwide supply, according to the International Energy Agency.

In economic news, the initial jobless claims barely budged in the past week. The U.S. trade deficit narrowed more than expected in January.

Also, housing starts unexpectedly rose in January, while building permits dropped more than consensus.

The Federal Reserve balance sheet will be released later in the day.

data.title: Occidental Petroleum jumps after winning two analyst upgrades
data.description:
Occidental Petroleum (OXY) +5.9% in Thursday's trading, rallying to its best level since August 2024 after Wells Fargo upgraded shares to Overweight from Underweight with a $69 price target, up from $47, while Piper Sandler raised the stock to Overweight from Neutral with a $66 PT, up from $54.

Wells Fargo's Sam Margolin double upgraded Occidental (OXY), saying the stock's peer-leading oil sensitivity is "both an opportunity and a risk," but stronger Permian Basin productivity and improved capital intensity now support a more constructive setup.

Margolin said his prior Sell-equivalent rating was driven by return of capital constraints due to preferred equity, which - barring a $4/share return of capital trigger - are unlikely to be redeemed until 2029, and while the constraint remains unless oil stays above $100/bbl, "the step change in capital efficiency reflected in 4Q25 earnings supports regular dividend growth and buyback opportunities in the intervening years."

Oxy (OXY) has adjusted its Permian spending plan from $3.9B to $3.1B while maintaining
production growth, which is enabled by a combination of factors including strong underlying productivity and enhanced oil recovery which dampens observable base decline, and the analyst said he believes the company can produce slightly ahead of guidance in 2026 and resume an accelerated growth trend in 2027 back to $3.5B in capital.

Piper Sandler's Mark Lear views Occidental (OXY) as one of the best operators in the Delaware Basin that continues to deliver among the best operating performance in the basin despite some
degradation in FY 2025, and noted the company's FY 2026 guidance was much more capital efficient than initial soft guidance, with ~$800M less capital to drive a similar level of production.

Despite strong YTD performance, Occidental (OXY) trades roughly in line with large-cap peers on estimated FY 2026 strip EBITDA and FCF/EV yield and demonstrates some of the strongest leverage to oil upside, Lear wrote.

data.title: Esquire Financial to acquire Signature Bancorporation in all-stock deal
data.description:
Esquire Financial Holdings (ESQ) is set to acquire Signature Bancorporation in an all-stock transaction.

ESQ shares were 6.69% higher to $105.67 during Thursday afternoon.

Signature shareholders will receive a fixed exchange ratio of 2.63 Esquire shares for each stock they own.

The company statement said the exchange ratio is subject to an adjustment based on the disposition value of certain Signature Bank loans. Signature has initiated a sale process for Schedule A Loans, with the disposition expected prior to the closing of the deal.

The per-share value equates to $260.48 for Signature shareholders based on the March 11 closing price of Esquire, bringing the aggregate transaction value to ~$348.4M.

The combined entity is expected to have ~$4.8B in assets at closing.

The acquisition is estimated to help Esquire expand into the Chicago market.

The transaction is estimated to be 23% accretive to the combined company's 2027 GAAP EPS, while no associated revenue enhancement is projected.

The transaction is ~11% accretive to Esquire's tangible book value, and assumes 5% cost savings.

The resulting company's board will consist of eleven directors, including nine directors from Esquire and two directors from Signature. Signature Board Chairman Leonard Caronia and CEO Michael O'Rourke are set to join the board of the merged entity.

The transaction is set to close in the third quarter.
